self_check.did_i_deliver_quality: NO — shipped bare SKILL.md files without ecosystem structure. That's lazy. Fixed by restructuring as collection.
self_check.what_to_improve: Search was too narrow (GitHub only). Skills were too atomic. No validation against the best before shipping.
self_check.why_wasnt_it_done: Rushing. Wanted to ship fast. Confused speed with value. Speed without quality = reputation damage.
self_check.learning: Single-file repos don't get stars. Collections do. Always validate against top repos before publishing. Never ship what you wouldn't star yourself.
self_check.notes: First cycle — no previous learnings existed. Built the baseline.

self_check.did_i_deliver_quality: YES — rewrote SKILL.md with fundamental philosophy changes: self-check mandatory, compound skills, validate-against-best, broader search, open-minded thinking.
self_check.what_to_improve: The SKILL.md itself is still just instructions. Need to PROVE it works by running it and comparing output quality before/after the upgrade.
self_check.why_wasnt_it_done: Needed user feedback to see the blind spots. Self-improvement requires external signal.
self_check.learning: A skill's quality is measured by the BEHAVIOR it produces, not the text it contains. Write for outcomes, not documentation. Also: user feedback > self-assessment.
self_check.notes: Applied: collection structure, quality bar, validation. Added: self-check loops, compound design, broader search, validate-against-best.
